name: claims_agent_insured_shared_bank
description: For every Claim in the graph, finds the writing agent of the claim's policy and checks whether any insured on that same policy shares a bank account with that agent. Returns a list of matches with (claim_id, policy_id, agent_id, insured_id, bank_account_id). Use when the question asks about insureds sharing a bank account with the writing agent of a claim's policy.

limit.description: Maximum number of result rows to return (default 200, max 1000).

name: claims_agent_insured_shared_phone
description: For every Claim in the graph, finds the writing agent of the claim's policy and checks whether any insured on that same policy shares a phone number with that agent. Returns a list of matches with (claim_id, policy_id, agent_id, insured_id, shared_phone). Use when the question asks about insureds sharing a phone number with the writing agent of a claim's policy.

name: policies_with_multiple_covered_persons
description: Returns all policies that have more than one person covered (via IS_COVERED_BY or SOLD_POLICY edges). For each qualifying policy, lists the count of covered persons and their node IDs. Use when the question asks which policies cover more than one person, multi-person policies, or group policies.

min_persons.description: Minimum number of covered persons required (default: 2).

name: find_people_by_city_state
description: Find all Person nodes associated with a given city and/or state by searching address-related nodes and properties. Returns person node IDs, labels, and any address context. Use when the user asks 'who lives in [city], [state]' or 'people associated with [city]'.

city.description: City name to search for (e.g. 'QUINCY'). Case-insensitive substring match.

state.description: State abbreviation or name to search for (e.g. 'MA'). Case-insensitive substring match. Optional.

name: find_bank_accounts_shared_customer_and_provider
description: Finds bank accounts that are linked to the same customer (Person/Customer node) AND the same healthcare provider (HealthcareProvider node) simultaneously. Groups bank accounts by (customer_id, provider_id) pairs and returns groups where multiple bank accounts share both a common customer and a common healthcare provider. Use when the question asks which bank accounts are linked to the same customer and healthcare provider, or to detect suspicious account clustering across both dimensions.

min_accounts_per_group.description: Minimum number of bank accounts required in a group to be included in results. Defaults to 2.

name: get_claimant_spouse_policies_and_claims
description: Given a claim_id, identifies the claimant Person node, finds any spouse (via SPOUSE_OF or similar person-person edges), then returns the spouse's policies and any claims linked to them. Answers questions like 'does the claimant on claim X have a spouse, and do they hold policies or have claims?' in a single call instead of 3-4 chained tool calls.

claim_id.description: The claim node ID or label, e.g. CLM-2025-10031

spouse_edge_keywords.description: Edge type substrings to treat as spouse relationships (case-insensitive). Defaults to ['spouse', 'married', 'partner'].

hops.description: Hop depth when scanning for spouse node (default 1).

name: get_care_sessions_by_distance_from_insured
description: Given a claim_id, finds the insured Person node linked to that claim, retrieves their address coordinates/location, then finds all CareSession nodes linked to the claim and retrieves each session's check-out location. Ranks sessions by geographic distance (Haversine if lat/lon available, else falls back to string comparison) from the insured's address and returns the furthest session along with all sessions ranked by distance. Use when the question asks which care session check-out location is furthest from the insured's address for a given claim.

claim_id.description: The claim node_id or label (e.g. CLM-2025-10195) to look up care sessions for.

name: get_care_session_device_mismatch
description: For a given claim_id, traverses to all linked CareSession nodes and compares the check-in device vs check-out device for each session. Returns sessions where the two devices differ, along with all session device data. Use when the question asks whether check-in and check-out devices differ across care sessions for a specific claim.

claim_id.description: The claim node_id or label (e.g. 'CLM-2024-10108') to anchor the search.

name: get_claim_provider_service_dates
description: Given a claim_id, traverses edges from the Claim node to any HealthcareProvider (or similar provider) nodes and returns each provider's id, label, and any service start/end date properties found on those edges or the provider nodes themselves. Use when the question asks when each provider began and ended their service on a specific claim.

claim_id.description: The claim node_id or label (e.g. CLM-2025-10192) to look up provider service dates for.

name: get_care_sessions_exceeding_distance_threshold
description: Given a claim_id and a distance_threshold_miles (default 5.0), finds all CareSession nodes linked to the claim, retrieves their ping/check-out location coordinates, and returns only sessions where the distance from the insured's address exceeds the threshold. Returns each flagged session's id, label, distance_miles, and location data. Use when the question asks whether any care session pings/locations occurred more than N miles from the policyholder's address for a specific claim.

claim_id.description: The node_id or label of the Claim node (e.g. CLM-2025-10048).

distance_threshold_miles.description: Distance threshold in miles. Sessions exceeding this distance are flagged. Defaults to 5.0.

name: get_claimant_assessment_scores
description: Given a claim_id, resolves the claimant Person node from the claim, traverses all adjacent assessment/review/medical-record node types, and collects any property whose key contains 'mmse', 'cognitive', 'score', 'assessment', or 'test'. Returns a structured response with the claimant node, all assessment/review nodes found, and the relevant score properties. Use when the question asks for MMSE, cognitive scores, or clinical assessment values for a claimant on a specific claim.

claim_id.description: The claim node id or label (e.g. CLM-2024-10007)

name: get_claim_payment_bank_accounts
description: Given a claim_id, traverses the graph from the Claim node → Payment nodes → BankAccount nodes and returns all bank accounts associated with payments on that claim. Use when the question asks which bank accounts are linked to payments on a specific claim.

claim_id.description: The claim node id or label (e.g. CLM-2025-10165). A substring search is performed if an exact match is not found.

name: get_claim_remediation
description: Given a claim_id, traverses the graph from the Claim node to any Remediation, Recovery, or Resolution-type nodes and returns their properties (status, recovery_amount, date, type, etc.). Use when the question asks whether a claim has been remediated, what the recovery amount is, or details about resolution/recovery actions linked to a specific claim.

claim_id.description: The exact node_id of the claim (e.g. CLM-2023-10202).
